comfyWorkflowEditor.title: Workflow setup
comfyWorkflowEditor.importLead: Import a ComfyUI workflow once, then map which node field plays each role. The setup is saved to this connection and reused for every generation.
comfyWorkflowEditor.importStep1: In ComfyUI, open Settings and turn on Enable Dev mode Options.
comfyWorkflowEditor.importStep2: Build your workflow, then use Save (API Format).
comfyWorkflowEditor.importStep3: Drop or paste that JSON here. The UI export is not supported.
comfyWorkflowEditor.importFailed: Import failed. Make sure it is the API-format export.
comfyWorkflowEditor.mapFailed: Could not save the mapping. Try again.
comfyWorkflowEditor.needsPositive: Map a positive prompt to continue
comfyWorkflowEditor.needsReimport: This workflow was imported in an older format. Replace it with a fresh API-format export.
comfyWorkflowEditor.unknownNodes: {{count}} node type(s) aren't recognized by the connected server: {{nodes}}. You can still map and save, but generation will fail unless those custom node packs are installed on this server.
comfyWorkflowEditor.customHint: Map any other node field, including custom or extension nodes, to expose it during generation.

profile.tabDescription: View and edit the active character

presets.tabDescription: Configure reasoning, chain-of-thought, and prompt behavior

loom.tabDescription: Configure narrative structure and story beats

characters.tabDescription: Browse and manage your character cards

lorebook.tabDescription: Edit world book and lorebook entries

cortex.tabDescription: View and manage memory cortex entries

databank.tabDescription: Upload and manage reference documents for AI context

create.tabDescription: Create and edit Lumia items and Loom presets

prompt.tabDescription: Pick Lumia and Loom content, Sovereign Hand, and context filters

council.tabDescription: Configure the Lumia Council and tool functions

feedback.tabDescription: View the latest council execution results

worldinfo.tabDescription: View currently activated world info entries

imagegen.tabDescription: Configure and control AI scene generation

wallpaper.tabDescription: Set global or per-chat background wallpapers

regex.tabDescription: Create and manage regex find/replace scripts

branches.tabDescription: View and navigate the chat branch history

theme.tabDescription: Customize colors, accent, and visual style

connectionManager.empty: No connections configured. Add one to start chatting.

promptPanel.loomContentDescPrefix: Select narrative styles, utilities, and retrofits from your loaded packs. These are injected via
promptPanel.sovereignHandDesc: Enable Sovereign Hand integration to use advanced prompt manipulation features.
promptPanel.useSovereignHandHint: Enables Sovereign Hand macros for advanced prompt control
promptPanel.excludeLastMessageHint: When enabled, removes the last user message from the outgoing context
promptPanel.includeMessageInMasterPromptHint: When enabled, includes the user message in the {{loomSovHand}} macro output
promptPanel.lastMessageName: returns the name of whoever sent the last message
promptPanel.loomContinuePrompt: adds continuation instructions when character spoke last
promptPanel.contextFiltersDesc: Filter out specific content from the chat context before sending to the AI.
promptPanel.keepHtmlDepth: Keep HTML in last N messages
promptPanel.keepFontsDepth: Keep fonts in last N messages
promptPanel.keepOnlyDetailsContentHint: Past the keep depth, discard everything except content inside <details> blocks
promptPanel.filterLoomTagsHint: Removes Lucid Loom-related tags from older messages
promptPanel.keepLoomTagsDepth: Keep Loom tags in last N messages
promptPanel.keepOnlyLoomContentHint: Past the keep depth, discard everything except content inside Loom-related tags

oocPanel.displayStyleHint: How OOC comments appear in chat

presetManager.bindingChangedHint: The panel has been changed locally. Edit the connection and use Capture Current to update the saved snapshot.
presetManager.autoParseThoughtsHint: Display only: strip thinking tags from message bubbles
presetManager.apiReasoningHint: Request native reasoning from the provider, if available
presetManager.thinkingDisplayDisabledHint: Only sent when API reasoning is enabled.
presetManager.thinkingDisplayHint: Opus 4.7/4.8 and Mythos Preview default to Omitted. Pick Summarized to receive summary text.
presetManager.keepAllReasoning: Keep all reasoning in prompt history
presetManager.stripAllReasoning: Strip all reasoning from prompt history
presetManager.keepLastBlocks: Keep last {{count}} blocks in prompt history
presetManager.keepInHistoryHint: Affects what gets sent back to the model. Message bubbles still follow Auto-parse thoughts.

personaManager.deleteFolderMessage: Delete folder "{{name}}"? Personas in this folder will move to Uncategorized.
personaManager.renamedFolderSuccess: Renamed folder to "{{name}}" and updated {{count}} personas
personaManager.deletedFolderSuccess: Deleted folder "{{name}}" and moved {{count}} personas to Uncategorized

connectionItem.signInOpenRouterHint: Sign in with OpenRouter to get an API key
connectionItem.signInNanoGptHint: Sign in with NanoGPT to get an API key

connectionForm.pollinationsSaved: Signed in with Pollinations. API key saved automatically.
connectionForm.pollinationsAutoSaveFailed: Pollinations sign-in succeeded, but auto-save failed. Click Save to persist manually.
connectionForm.pollinationsCaptured: Signed in with Pollinations. API key captured. Click Create to save this connection.
connectionForm.nanoGptSaved: Signed in with NanoGPT. API key saved automatically.
connectionForm.nanoGptExchangeFailed: NanoGPT sign-in failed during key exchange
connectionForm.invalidServiceAccountMissingFields: Invalid service account JSON: missing required fields (private_key, client_email, project_id)
connectionForm.invalidJsonFile: Invalid JSON file. Please upload a valid Google service account key file.
connectionForm.name: Name
connectionForm.credentialsLoaded: Credentials loaded{{file}}. Upload a new file to replace.
connectionForm.uploadServiceAccount: Upload your Google Cloud service account key JSON file
connectionForm.vertexRegionHint: Google Cloud region for Vertex AI
connectionForm.bedrockEndpointHint: Mantle is recommended (broadest model catalog). Use Runtime for cross-region inference profiles like us.anthropic.claude-*.
connectionForm.bedrockApiKeyHint: Create a long- or short-term API key in the Amazon Bedrock console. It's used as a bearer token — no AWS SigV4 signing needed.
connectionForm.pollinationsByopHint: Use Sign in with Pollinations to fetch a BYOP key automatically, or paste a key manually below.
connectionForm.nanoGptOAuthHint: Use Sign in with NanoGPT to create an app-specific API key automatically, or paste a key manually below.
connectionForm.keyAlreadySet: Key is set. Enter a new value to replace it.
connectionForm.vertexApiUrlHint: Leave empty to use default Vertex AI endpoint with selected region
connectionForm.defaultApiUrlHint: Leave empty for default provider URL
connectionForm.modelHint: Refresh uses the current form values, even before the connection is saved.
connectionForm.useSubscriptionApiHint: Use /api/subscription/v1 to only use models from your NanoGPT subscription
connectionForm.enableNanoGptCachingHint: Sends NanoGPT's prompt_caching helper for Claude routes. Non-Claude models (GLM, GPT, Gemini, etc.) keep using NanoGPT's automatic implicit caching — no flags needed and subscription routing stays intact.
connectionForm.nanoGptPromptCacheTtlHint: How long NanoGPT should retain the cached prefix. Use 1 hour for slower flows at higher write cost.
connectionForm.stickyProviderHint: Prefer the previously recorded upstream provider for cache hits. NanoGPT returns 503 on failover instead of switching providers, preserving cache integrity.
connectionForm.useCodingPlanEndpointHint: Use /api/coding/paas/v4 for Z.AI Coding Plan access instead of the general /api/paas/v4 endpoint
connectionForm.enableAnthropicCachingHint: Automatically cache prompts to reduce cost and latency for repetitive prefixes
connectionForm.anthropicPromptCacheTtlHint: Anthropic defaults to a 5-minute cache. Use 1 hour for slower follow-up flows at higher write cost.
connectionForm.useAutomaticCachingHint: Apply Anthropic's top-level automatic cache breakpoint to the last eligible block.
connectionForm.explicitCacheBreakpointsHint: Add Anthropic block-level breakpoints on request sections that stay stable across calls.
connectionForm.bindReasoningSettingsHint: Save current reasoning settings (including "Start Reply With") and auto-apply when this connection is selected
connectionForm.snapshotAlreadyMatches: Snapshot already matches the current reasoning settings
connectionForm.replaceSavedSnapshot: Replace the saved snapshot with the current reasoning settings
connectionForm.currentValuesDiffer: Current panel values differ from this connection's saved snapshot.

summaryEditor.clearConfirm: Clear the summary for this chat?
summaryEditor.modeAutoHint: Summaries are generated automatically at set intervals.
summaryEditor.modeManualHint: Click the Generate button to create summaries on demand.
summaryEditor.sidecarHint: Uses the shared Sidecar connection configured in your settings.
summaryEditor.activeConnectionHint: Uses whichever connection profile is currently active.
summaryEditor.dedicatedConnectionHint: Uses a specific connection profile for all summarization.
summaryEditor.noConnectionProfiles: No connection profiles configured. Create one in the Connections panel.
summaryEditor.timeoutHint: Milliseconds to wait for summary generation
summaryEditor.defaultTimeout: Default is {{value}} ms. Increase this for slower models or larger summary contexts.
summaryEditor.messageCountHint: Keep the N most recent messages during generation
summaryEditor.trimmedWithSummary: Older messages will be trimmed from context. Use {{loomSummary}} in your preset to retain context from the summary.
summaryEditor.trimmedWithoutSummary: Older messages will be trimmed from context. Enable summarization to preserve context from older messages via the {{loomSummary}} macro.
summaryEditor.promptTemplateHint: Customize how the model is instructed to produce summaries. Leave blank to use the server defaults. These placeholders are substituted at generation time:
summaryEditor.rebuildModalMessage: This will process all {{totalMessages}} messages in {{batchCount}} batch(es). The existing summary will be preserved if the rebuild fails midway.

regexPanel.title: Regex Scripts

regexPanel.selectPresetFirstFolder: Select a Loom preset before binding regex folders
regexPanel.slowDisplayTimedOut: Display regex "{{name}}" timed out and was flagged in Regex Scripts.
regexPanel.slowDisplaySlow: Display regex "{{name}}" ran for {{duration}} and was flagged in Regex Scripts.
regexPanel.name: Name
regexPanel.timedOutDetail: This script hit the regex timeout and was skipped during execution.
regexPanel.slowDetail: This script was flagged as slow after taking {{seconds}}s to run.
regexPanel.findHint: Regex pattern - use (parentheses) to capture groups
regexPanel.replaceHint: What matched text becomes - supports HTML
regexPanel.replacePlaceholder: Leave empty to delete matches, or use $1, $& and HTML

personaEditor.tagAutoBindHint: Exact character bindings override these rules. Any/All only differs once 2+ tags are selected.
personaEditor.chooseTwoTags: Choose at least two tags to use Any vs All matching.
personaEditor.snapshotAddonStates: Snapshot current add-on states into this tag binding
personaEditor.selectTagsHint: Select tags to auto-switch this persona for matching characters.
personaEditor.activeMatchesWithConflict: {{name}} matches, but {{count}} other personas also match.
personaEditor.currentChatPrefersExact: Current chat still prefers its exact character binding.

imageGenPanel.enableHint: Generate scene-aware chat backgrounds through the council scene tool
imageGenPanel.exportIncludeParametersHint: Steps, CFG, size, seed and other parameters of the active connection. Applied on import to the active connection when the provider matches.
imageGenPanel.exportIncludeConnectionsHint: Connection parameters, workflows and server URLs. API keys are never exported.
imageGenPanel.importSuccess: Image generation config imported ({{presets}} presets, {{connections}} connections)
imageGenPanel.createConnectionFirst: Create a connection in the Connections tab first
imageGenPanel.modeHint: Scene analyzes chat into a scene prompt. Custom sends your prompt directly. Chat-aware custom uses your instructions to rewrite the current chat context into the final image prompt.
imageGenPanel.outputHint: Choose whether the result becomes the chat background, inserted as a new chat image, or attached to the latest existing message.
imageGenPanel.editingHint: Switch which preset bucket the text fields below are editing. Main feeds the live prompt; Character/Persona define snippets that replace {{character_prompt}} / {{persona_prompt}} at generation time.
imageGenPanel.pickMainPresetHint: Pick a saved main preset to load it into the editor below. It also becomes the active prompt sent at generation time.
imageGenPanel.pickCharacterPresetHint: Pick a character preset to load it into the editor and bind it to the current chat's character.
imageGenPanel.openChatBindPreset: Open a chat to bind a preset to its character. Until then, picks won't persist.
imageGenPanel.pickPersonaPresetHint: Pick a persona preset to load it into the editor and bind it to the active persona.
imageGenPanel.selectActivePersona: Select an active persona to bind a preset to it.
imageGenPanel.pickCaptioningPresetHint: Pick a saved captioning preset to load it. These presets define how the Image Captioner describes uploaded images.
imageGenPanel.parserInstructionsHint: Instructions for how the parser LLM should turn chat context into the final image prompt. This is not sent directly to the image provider.
imageGenPanel.sentDirectlyHint: Sent directly to the image provider.
imageGenPanel.characterSnippetHint: Text spliced in wherever {{character_prompt}} appears in the main preset.
imageGenPanel.personaSnippetHint: Text spliced in wherever {{persona_prompt}} appears in the main preset.
imageGenPanel.captioningInstructionsHint: Instructions sent alongside the uploaded image to the parser LLM in the Image Captioner modal.
imageGenPanel.parserPromptExample: Example: Focus on the current pose, expressions, clothing, lighting, and room details. Use concise image-generation tags.
imageGenPanel.describeImage: Describe the image you want to generate...
imageGenPanel.characterPromptMacroHint: will be replaced with the bound character preset at generation time.
imageGenPanel.personaPromptMacroHint: will be replaced with the bound persona preset at generation time.
imageGenPanel.negativeSnippetHint: Replaces the matching negative macro in the main preset's negative prompt.
imageGenPanel.parserConnectionHint: Overrides the Council sidecar for ImageGen scene/prompt parsing.
imageGenPanel.noModelsReturned: No models returned. Refresh, or enter one manually.
imageGenPanel.selectConnectionError: Select an image gen connection to generate backgrounds.
imageGenPanel.failedUpdateCharacterBinding: Failed to update character preset binding
imageGenPanel.failedUpdatePersonaBinding: Failed to update persona preset binding
imageGenPanel.openChatFirst: Open a chat first to generate a scene background.
imageGenPanel.noMessageToAttach: No message to attach the generated image to.
imageGenPanel.failedLoadReferences: Failed to load one or more reference images
imageGenPanel.noModelsFound: No models found. Refresh, or enter one manually.
imageGenPanel.promptGenerationTimeoutHint: Seconds to wait for ImageGen scene parsing or parsed custom prompt generation. Set to 0 to disable.
imageGenPanel.imageGenerationTimeoutHint: Seconds to wait for the image provider after the prompt is ready. Increase this for long ComfyUI workflows, or set to 0 to disable.
imageGenPanel.importWorkflowHint: Import a ComfyUI workflow JSON and map prompt, seed, sampler, size, and model fields for generation.
imageGenPanel.exposedFromWorkflow: Exposed from the imported ComfyUI workflow.
imageGenPanel.rawOverrideInvalidJson: Invalid JSON — generation will fail until this is fixed or cleared
imageGenPanel.rawOverrideNotObject: Must be a JSON object of request fields, e.g. {"steps": 30}
imageGenPanel.includeCharacterAvatarHint: Send current character avatar as director reference
imageGenPanel.includePersonaAvatarHint: Send persona avatar as director reference
imageGenPanel.includeCharactersPersonaHint: Adds character and active persona descriptions to scene parsing, and asks the parser to include visible subjects when supported by the chat context.
imageGenPanel.previewBeforeGenerateHint: When on, clicking Generate runs the parser and opens an editable preview of the outgoing prompt before sending it to the image provider.
imageGenPanel.recycleGeneratedImagesHint: When off, ImageGen chat attachments stay visible in chat but are not re-sent to the LLM.
imageGenPanel.addGeneratedToGallery: Add Generated Images to Character Gallery
imageGenPanel.addGeneratedToGalleryHint: When on, every generated image is also linked into the active chat's character gallery. Turn off to keep generations out of the gallery.
imageGenPanel.generatedImagesResendHint: Only the most recent generated images are included in multimodal context.
imageGenPanel.contextMessageLimitHint: Number of most recent chat messages sent to the prompt parser for scene analysis and parsed custom prompts.

characterEditor.personaCreateNoContent: Add a description or personality before creating a persona.
characterEditor.personaAvatarCopyFailed: Persona created, but the avatar could not be copied.
characterEditor.appearanceHelper: Appearance data for Dream Weaver characters.

characterEditor.personalityHelper: Key personality traits and behavioral patterns.
characterEditor.scenarioHelper: The setting or situation for the roleplay.
characterEditor.voiceGuidanceHelper: Structured voice rules are used at runtime first, compiled guidance remains the fallback.
characterEditor.systemPromptHelper: Instructions injected at the start of every conversation.
characterEditor.postHistoryHelper: Instructions injected after the chat history (jailbreak position).
characterEditor.firstMessageHelper: The opening message the character sends when starting a new chat.
characterEditor.messageExamplesHelper: Example dialogues showing how the character speaks (use <START> to separate).
characterEditor.alternateGreetingsHelper: Alternative first messages that can be randomly selected.
characterEditor.alternateNameHelper: Used as {{char}} in prompts and as the chat display name. Leaves the card's title untouched.
characterEditor.creator: Creator
characterEditor.creatorNotesHelper: Notes from the creator about how to use the character.
characterEditor.imageGalleryHelper: Upload images for this character. These are personal to your account.
characterEditor.openChatForBackground: Open a chat first to set its background
characterEditor.attachedWorldBooksHelper: Used by prompt assembly for world info activation. Attach multiple for richer lore.
characterEditor.lorebookImported: Imported {{count}} entries into "{{name}}" and attached it
characterEditor.characterRegexScriptsHelper: Regex scripts attached to this character. These will be bundled with .charx exports.
characterEditor.noRegexScripts: No regex scripts found. Create one in the Regex panel first.
characterEditor.bindRegexPlaceholder: Bind a global regex to this character…
characterEditor.extensionsJsonHelper: Raw JSON data for character extensions and custom fields.
characterEditor.deleteCharacterMessage: Delete "{{name}}" permanently? This cannot be undone.
characterEditor.characterVoiceHelper: Default voice used when this character speaks. Falls back to the global voice when unset. Group chats can override this per chat.

expressionEditor.title: Expression Sprites
expressionEditor.titleHelper: Map emotion labels to images for visual novel-style expression display during chat.
expressionEditor.titleMultiHelper: This card contains expressions for {{count}} characters ({{total}} total). The active character is detected automatically from conversation context.
expressionEditor.detectionHintMulti: After each message, the system identifies which character is speaking and selects their expression.
expressionEditor.detectionHintSingle: How should the character's expression be chosen after each message?

auto.name: Automatic
auto.descSingle: A lightweight sidecar LLM call runs after each generation to detect the expression. Works without Council mode.
auto.descMulti: A sidecar LLM call identifies the active character and their expression after each generation.

council.name: Council Tool Only
council.desc: Expression detection runs as part of the Council deliberation. Requires Council mode enabled with the Expression Detector tool assigned to a member.

off.name: Off
off.descMulti: No automatic detection. Expressions only change via <img=""> tags in the response.
off.descSingle: No automatic detection. Expressions only change via the Council tool (if assigned) or stay on the default.

expressionEditor.noModelsReturned: No models returned. Enter one manually.
expressionEditor.sidecarFallbackHint: Falls back to the sidecar LLM configured in the Council panel.
expressionEditor.emptyHintMulti: Upload a ZIP of expression images, add from gallery, or upload one or many image files.
expressionEditor.emptyHintSingle: Upload a ZIP of expression images (filenames become labels), add images from the gallery, or upload one or many image files.
expressionEditor.noGalleryImages: No gallery images found. Upload images to the Gallery tab first.
expressionEditor.expressionLabelPrompt: Enter an expression label for this image:
expressionEditor.newGroupPrompt: Enter a character name for the new expression group:
expressionEditor.deleteLabelConfirmMessage: Delete the expression "{{label}}"? This cannot be undone.
expressionEditor.deleteGroupConfirmMessage: Remove the group "{{name}}" and all of its expressions? This cannot be undone.

imageLora.activeConnectionHelper: The LoRA list below is pulled from this connection. To change it, switch the active connection in the Image Gen panel.
imageLora.loraHelper: Spliced into the active ComfyUI workflow's LoraLoader node, or sent as a SwarmUI loras/loraweights parameter. Other providers will ignore this field but still see the base tags below.
imageLora.discoveryHint: LoRA discovery is only available for ComfyUI and SwarmUI connections. You can still type a filename manually below.
imageLora.noLorasOnConnection: No LoRAs found on the active connection
imageLora.savedValueSublabel: Not in current connection — saved value
imageLora.strengthHelper: Typical range is 0–1. ComfyUI applies each strength separately; SwarmUI uses the model strength only.
imageLora.baseTagsHelper: Prepended to every image prompt for this character. Booru-style tags work best (lowercase, underscores).
imageLora.baseTagsPlaceholder: 1girl, long brown hair, pink dress, green eyes
imageLora.sourceUrlHelper: Travels with PNG exports as a display-only hint. Lumiverse will never auto-download from this URL.
imageLora.clearConfirmMessage: Remove the saved LoRA binding from this character? This cannot be undone.

characterBrowser.importFromUrlHint: Paste one character URL per line from Chub, JannyAI, BotBooru, or other supported sources.

tagFilter.label: Tags
tagFilter.cycleHint: Click to include, again to exclude, again to clear.

alternateAvatars.helper: Additional profile pictures for this character, switchable per chat.
alternateAvatars.selectionHint: Tap an avatar to use it in this chat.

toast.loraHint: {{name}} expects LoRA "{{filename}}" @ {{weight}}. Configure it in the character editor's Image LoRA tab.{{sourceHint}}

worldBookPanel.openChatToAdd: Open a chat to add chat-scoped world books
worldBookPanel.noChatActive: No world books active for this chat

entries.switchAllDrag: Switch page size to All entries to drag-reorder without pagination.
entries.renumberHint: Applies numbering in the current list order.

entryEditor.countTokensTitle: Estimate token count using current model's tokenizer
entryEditor.recursionInactiveNote: Vectorized entries do not participate in recursive keyword chaining. Semantic retrieval uses indexed content directly.

vectorStatus.off: Vector activation is off for this entry.
vectorStatus.indexedReady: Indexed and ready for vector activation.
vectorStatus.disabledEntry: This entry is disabled, so it will not be indexed until it is enabled again.
vectorStatus.needsContent: This entry needs content before it can be indexed.
vectorStatus.reindexNeeded: Reindex this book after vector changes so this entry can be searched.

worldBookPanel.name: Name
worldBookPanel.selectOrCreate: Select a book or create a new one
worldBookPanel.deleteWorldBookMessage: Delete this book and all its entries? This cannot be undone.
worldBookPanel.deleteEntryMessage: Delete this entry? This cannot be undone.
worldBookPanel.convertNoneEligible: No entries are eligible for conversion. All non-constant entries are either already vectorized with no trigger keywords, empty, or disabled.
worldBookPanel.convertIntro_one: This will enable vector activation for {{count}} entry, clear its primary and secondary trigger keywords, and immediately start reindexing.
worldBookPanel.convertIntro_other: This will enable vector activation for {{count}} entries, clear their primary and secondary trigger keywords, and immediately start reindexing.
worldBookPanel.convertKeysCleared_one: {{count}} entry has trigger keywords that will be cleared
worldBookPanel.convertKeysCleared_other: {{count}} entries have trigger keywords that will be cleared
worldBookPanel.convertKeysRetained_one: {{count}} entry has trigger keywords that will be retained for hybrid activation
worldBookPanel.convertKeysRetained_other: {{count}} entries have trigger keywords that will be retained for hybrid activation
worldBookPanel.globalScanDepthHint: Default scan depth for entries without a per-entry setting. Controls how many recent messages are scanned for keywords.
worldBookPanel.maxRecursionPassesHint: How many recursive keyword-chaining passes to run. 0 disables recursion entirely.
worldBookPanel.maxActivatedEntriesHint: Cap the total number of activated entries per generation. 0 = unlimited. Highest-priority entries survive; constants are never evicted.
worldBookPanel.maxTokenBudgetHint: Approximate max WI content in tokens. 0 = unlimited. Entries included in priority order until budget is met.
worldBookPanel.minPriorityThresholdHint: Entries below this priority are excluded entirely. Constants are exempt. 0 = no filter.

scoreGuide.title: How to read these scores
scoreGuide.body: Vector distance is the raw embedding distance, so lower is better. Rerank score is the final composite ranking after boosts and penalties, so higher is better.
scoreGuide.lexical: Lexical candidate score is an optional keyword/FTS-side signal used during reranking. Higher means stronger lexical support when it appears.
scoreGuide.cutoff: Similarity Threshold filters on vector distance before reranking. Rerank Cutoff filters on rerank score after reranking.

outcome.blocked_by_group_one: {{count}} was blocked by a group rule
outcome.blocked_by_group_other: {{count}} were blocked by a group rule
outcome.blocked_by_max_entries_one: {{count}} had no room under the entry cap
outcome.blocked_by_max_entries_other: {{count}} had no room under the entry cap
outcome.blocked_by_token_budget_one: {{count}} had no room under the token budget
outcome.blocked_by_token_budget_other: {{count}} had no room under the token budget

match.vectorOnly: This entry reached the shortlist mostly on vector similarity.

worldBookDiagnostics.headerTitle: Why "{{name}}" did or did not inject
worldBookDiagnostics.headerSubtitle: This view checks attachment, vector readiness, the query built from recent chat context, reranked vector matches, and what finally survived into the prompt.

hero.checkingBody: Looking at attachment, embeddings, reranked matches, and final prompt outcome.
hero.noDataBody: Run diagnostics again to inspect this chat.
hero.notAttachedTitle: This lorebook is not attached to the current chat
hero.notAttachedBody: Vector retrieval cannot inject anything until the book is attached through the character, persona, or global books.
hero.embeddingsNotReadyBody: Vector search is gated until embeddings are enabled, a key is configured, dimensions are known, and world-book vectorization is on.
hero.noEligibleTitle: This book has no vector-ready entries
hero.noEligibleBody: No non-empty, vector-enabled entries are eligible for retrieval in this lorebook.
hero.vectorActivatedTitle_one: {{count}} vector entry made the final prompt
hero.vectorActivatedTitle_other: {{count}} vector entries made the final prompt
hero.vectorActivatedBody: Retrieval pulled {{pulled}} candidates, {{cleared}} cleared the rerank cutoff, and {{activated}} survived into the final world-info result.
hero.noMatchesBody: The current chat query did not produce any vector hits that survived thresholding and reranking.
hero.keywordOverlapTitle: Vector retrieval mostly confirmed entries already hit by keywords
hero.keywordOverlapBody: The vector shortlist overlaps with keyword matches, so vector search did not add anything new for this chat.
hero.displacedTitle: Vector matches were found, but they did not survive final prompt assembly
hero.displacedBody: Retrieval pulled {{pulled}} candidates. {{fresh}} fresh vector candidate(s) made the shortlist, but {{displaced}} were displaced before the final prompt. {{why}}
hero.displacedWhyFallback: Open the reranked shortlist below to see which entries were displaced and why.
hero.noVectorActivatedTitle: Vector retrieval found candidates, but none became vector-activated entries
hero.noVectorActivatedBody: The reranked shortlist exists, but the final prompt still ended up with zero vector-only additions.

metrics.attachHint: Attach through the character, persona, or global books.

sections.noVectorHits: No vector hits survived the threshold and rerank steps for this chat.
sections.noPulledSummary: No candidates were pulled from vector search for this chat.
sections.pulledSummaryFallback: Open to inspect every pulled entry and why it stayed or got dropped.
sections.noPulledCandidates: No vector candidates were pulled for this chat.
sections.searchPlaceholder: Search pulled entries, titles, aliases, outcomes, or indexed text
sections.searchNoMatch: No pulled vector candidates match the current search.
sections.noQuery: No recent visible chat messages were available to build a vector query.
sections.noBlockers: No obvious blockers were reported for this chat.

worldBookDiagnostics.overlapSummary: {{overlap}} of {{total}} vector matches were already activated by keyword logic.
worldBookDiagnostics.noKeywordMatches: No keyword matches were reported for this chat.

settings.interceptorHint: How long to wait for an extension's pre-generation interceptor to finish. Higher values let extensions do heavier retrieval or context assembly before the LLM call at the cost of a longer delay before generation starts. Range {{min}}–{{max}}s (default {{default}}s). Individual extensions can override this via their manifest.
settings.dockHint: Side-mounted extension dock panels follow this desktop preference. On mobile, those same dock panels always collapse into a top sheet so they do not compete with the chat input area.
settings.toastDock: Extension dock panels will open on the {{side}}

spindlePanel.updateAllConfirmMessage: Each extension will fetch and rebuild from its remote. This can take a while — you can keep using the app while it runs, and progress will show in this panel. Any currently-disabled extensions will still be updated but will stay disabled.

tools.sidecarHint: Tools run on the sidecar LLM configured above. Each tool gets its own call.
tools.inlineHint: Tools are registered as function calls directly with the main LLM. No separate sidecar model is used — the primary model handles tool invocations itself. Requires Enable Function Calling in your Loom preset's completion settings.
tools.inlineWarning: Function Calling is disabled in the active Loom preset. Inline tools will not be sent.

hint.narrative_style: Define a writing style, tone, or narrative voice for the AI to adopt.

hint.retrofit: Retrofit content to adjust or transform existing narratives.

placeholder.narrative_style: Write in a poetic, flowing style with rich metaphors...
placeholder.loom_utility: When the user mentions combat, use detailed action descriptions...
placeholder.retrofit: Adjust the tone to be more lighthearted and casual...

toolEditor.helpText: Pack tools send a focused prompt to the sidecar during council deliberation. The prompt is the main instruction. Structured fields are optional, and help you define the sections or values you want the tool to return.
toolEditor.helpStep1: 1. Write the prompt like instructions for a specialist council member.
toolEditor.helpStep2: 2. Add structured fields only if you want a repeatable response shape.
toolEditor.helpStep3: 3. Use a result variable if you want the raw result in {{macroExample}}.
toolEditor.toolNameHint: Internal identifier, usually unique within the pack. Use snake_case.
toolEditor.displayNameHint: The label council editors will see.
toolEditor.descriptionHint: Explain when this tool should be used and what kind of guidance it gives.
toolEditor.promptHint: This is the main instruction the council member follows. Be explicit about scope, tone, and what the final answer should contain.
toolEditor.promptPlaceholder: You are a council specialist who reviews the latest story context and returns concise, actionable guidance...
toolEditor.promptHelperText: For pack-created council tools, the fields below do not replace your prompt. If you want a structured response, tell the model to return those fields explicitly.
toolEditor.structuredWhatText: Add fields for the pieces of information you want back from the tool. Use `string` for most text, `boolean` for yes or no, `integer` for whole numbers, and `number` for decimals. Leave this empty if the tool should answer in freeform prose.
toolEditor.fieldDescPlaceholder: What should go in this field?
toolEditor.resultRoutingText: Store in deliberation to let the whole council read this tool's output. Add a result variable when you also want the raw output available to prompts and presets through {{macroExample}}.
toolEditor.resultVariableHint: Optional. Saves the tool output under a macro-friendly name like scene_data or continuity_notes.
toolEditor.storeInDeliberationHint: Turn this off for variable-only tools whose output should be consumed by macros or other logic without appearing in the shared council deliberation block.
toolEditor.promptGuideFreeform: Leave this section empty if you want a freeform council response.
toolEditor.promptGuideIntro: Suggested instruction to add to your prompt:
toolEditor.promptGuideReturn: Return a concise response using these fields:
toolEditor.promptGuideFooter: Keep each field direct and easy for the council to use.

packBrowser.repairGenderMessage: Convert this pack from Lumiverse's older gender enum (0=Any, 1=Feminine, 2=Masculine) to the current enum (0=Feminine, 1=Masculine, 2=Neutral, 3=Any)? Use this only for packs created or edited under the old Lumiverse mapping.
packBrowser.deleteItemMessage: Delete this item? This cannot be undone.
packBrowser.deletePackMessage: Delete this pack and all its items? This cannot be undone.

blockEditor.title: Edit Block
blockEditor.name: Name
blockEditor.contentPlaceholder: Enter prompt content... Use {{macros}} for dynamic content.
blockEditor.sealedBlockHint: Advanced publishing option. When this preset is uploaded to LumiHub, the block content is removed from public JSON and stored as a private Hub-side block. Local copies still contain the text until upload.
blockEditor.sealedBlockInstalledHint: Installed from LumiHub. You can view and edit this block locally, but Lumiverse always strips this content from exports and share payloads.
blockEditor.sealedBlockKeyHint: Stable resolver key. Use letters, numbers, dots, dashes, underscores, or colons.
blockEditor.sealedBlockEnable: I understand this is for LumiHub publishing and want this block sealed on upload.
blockEditor.sealedBlockInstalledEnable: Always sealed for export because this block was installed from LumiHub.
blockEditor.categoryModeHint: Applies to the blocks inside this category. Ungrouped blocks and categories left on normal toggles behave exactly as they do now.
blockEditor.triggersNone: No triggers selected — block fires on all generation types

settings.resetAll: Reset all sampler overrides to defaults

settings.streamHint: Disable to receive the full response at once instead of token-by-token
settings.customBodyHint: Keys are spread onto the request body.
settings.assistantPrefillHint: Claude only — prepended to assistant response
settings.namesHint: Controls how speaker names are represented when formatting messages, including collapsed mode.
settings.stopHint: Appended to the request stop sequences
settings.collapseHint: Merges all prompt blocks and chat history into one user message. Use with "Names in Messages: In Content" for turn separation.
settings.emptySendNudgeHint: Injected when nudging for a fresh reply from an assistant-ending chat
settings.sendIfEmptyHint: Sent as a user message when the final assistant content is blank
